name: Machu Picchu: The Lost City of The Inca
description: The intricate stonework of Machu Picchu serves as a testament to Incan engineers.

name: Q'eswachaka Rope Bridge
description: Keshwa chaca, among the last handwoven Incan bridges, crosses Apurimac Canyon in Peru.

name: Rainbow Mountain
description: A remote candy-striped mountain in the Peruvian Andes straight out of the pages of Dr. Seuss.

name: Convento de San Francisco Ossuary
description: The mortal remains of an estimated 70,000 people lie in the basement of a monastery in Lima, Peru.

name: The Boiling River of the Amazon
description: This scalding hot river was thought to be a myth until one geoscientist made it his quest to study the mystical waters.

name: Mercado de Brujas
description: A market for witches is hidden within a train station.

name: Nazca Lines
description: A set of ancient drawings in the desert of Peru that are so massive, many of them can only be fully seen from the sky.

name: Ollantaytambo Ruins
description: Preserved Inca city and site of the last Inca stronghold against the Spanish conquistadors.

name: Huayllay National Sanctuary
description: A remarkable geological formation resembling a forest made of stone.

name: Gocta Waterfall
description: One of the world's tallest waterfalls wasn't revealed to the world at large until 2006.

name: Chauchilla Cemetery
description: Plundered and left asunder by grave robbers, this ancient necropolis has been painstakingly pieced back together.

name: Huacachina
description: An unexpected desert oasis in South America.

name: The Room of 10,000 Ancient Skulls
description: Lima’s Museum of Anthropology is lined with thousands of elongated human craniums.

name: Moray
description: Huge stone depressions which may have acted as an Incan agricultural experiment.

name: Huayna Picchu
description: If you’ve ever seen a picture of Machu Picchu, then you’ve seen a picture of Huayna Picchu—you just didn’t realize it.

name: Salt Pans of Maras
description: This Peruvian canyon is filled with geometric salt pools that have been being harvested since the Incan empire.

name: Skylodge
description: Sleep in a glass pod dangling off a cliff.

name: Sacsayhuamán
description: The stones of this Incan wall complex fit together so well you couldn't fit a piece of paper between some of them.

name: Cahuachi
description: Nazca pilgrimage site covering 370 acres is a popular stop for archeologists and looters.

name: Cotahuasi Canyon
description: With nearly double the depth of the Grand Canyon, this Peruvian canyon is one of the deepest in the world.

name: The Uros People of Lake Titicaca
description: The Uros people live on floating reed islands in lake Titicaca.

name: Sarcofagi of Carajía
description: These giant mummies are set ominously into a Peruvian cliffside and topped with real human skulls.

name: Yumbilla Falls
description: The world's fifth tallest waterfall is a spectacular silver chute of water that cuts through the cloud forest in northern Peru.

name: Qenqo Temple
description: Death rituals and sacrifice inside a Peruvian megalith.

name: Wiñay Wayna
description: These steeply terraced ruins are a stop along the Inca Trail to Machu Picchu.

name: Museo de la Coca (Coca Museum)
description: A fascinating little museum all about the rich history of Peru’s famous plant.

name: Pachacamac
description: The remains of what was the most important religious complex in coastal Peru for over a millennium.

name: Huaca Pucllana
description: This stone complex marks the urban center of a lost civilization.

name: Inca Bridge
description: A backdoor to Machu Picchu that only the most foolhardy infiltrator would attempt to breach.

name: Cueva de Las Lechuzas
description: A jungle cave in Peru of unknown depths, otherworldly sounds, and strange nocturnal birds that navigate by echolocation.

name: Brain Museum
description: Tucked behind the Institute of Neurological Science down a decrepit street resides the largest display of human brains in Latin America.

name: Huayllay Stone Forest
description: Extraordinary rock formations in open Peruvian fields.

name: Rodadero Slides
description: Did Inca children also slide down these smooth rocky slopes above Cusco?

name: Choquequirao, Peru
description: White llama terraces dazzle in Peru's "other Machu Picchu."

name: San Pedro Cemetery of Ninacaca
description: A colorful Peruvian city of the dead.

name: Bosque el Olivar (Olive Grove Forest)
description: A pretty park of over 1,600 olive trees that grew from just three saplings brought over from Spain.

name: Pizarro's Tomb
description: Now containing the bones of the actual conquistador, instead of someone else.

name: Chuncho Macaw Clay Lick
description: The world's largest clay lick, where parrots gather to snack.

name: The Festival of Paucartambo
description: Ain't no party like a ghost town party.

name: Gran Hotel Bolivar
description: Visiting dignitaries and Hollywood stars once stayed at this 1920s hotel, alongside ghosts that are said to haunt its corridors.

name: Larco Museum's Erotic Art
description: The large collection of pre-Columbian pottery might make you blush.

name: Marcahuasi Ruins
description: A mysterious pre-Incan ruin believed to have miraculous healing powers.

name: Tambopata Research Center
description: One of the most remote lodges in the Amazon offers rare wildlife encounters and undisturbed views of the Peruvian rainforest.

name: Chinchilla Last Supper
description: A painting in the historic Cathedral Basilica depicts Jesus and his disciples dining on a traditional Peruvian menu.

name: Paddington Bear of Lima
description: The little bear in the "funny kind of hat" has made his way from London back to Peru.

name: Huascarán National Park
description: The highest tropical mountain range in the world is in Central Peru, home to a vast array of exotic and quirky plants and animals.

name: Cerro Blanco
description: This unimaginably large pile of sand near Nazca is one of the tallest dunes in the world.

name: The Twelve-Angled Stone
description: This geometric feat is a testament to the Incas' mesmerizing architectural abilities.

name: Cusco Cathedral
description: Situated on a sacred Inca site, this 16th-century cathedral took nearly 100 years to build.

name: Gran Pajaten
description: These delicate ruins need more tourists to keep them from fading away.

name: Pongo de Mainique
description: A paradisical gorge with rapids, waterfalls, and wildlife whose raw beauty is the stuff of CGI movies.

name: Kuelap Fortress
description: An Archaeological Wonder of Peru, and the largest stone ruin site in the New World.

name: Nazca Lines Observation Tower
description: This metal tower in the desert provides views of a handful of Nazca geoglyphs.

name: Tomb of Enrique Torres Belón
description: This Peruvian bone silo lined with hanging human skeletons and hundreds of skulls is topped with a remarkable Michelangelo replica.

name: Paracas Candelabra
description: The mysterious "Candelabra of the Andes" could represent anything from a god's trident to a hallucinogenic plant.

name: Usko Ayar
description: Art school in the Amazon: Drugs, nature and sustainable agriculture.

name: Mummies of Museo Leymebamba
description: Hundreds of mummies in a silent room, some with eerily pained expressions preserved on their faces.

name: Huánuco Pampa
description: This remarkably well-preserved Incan city managed to survive the conquistadores.

name: Tambomachay
description: An Inca spa, military outpost or maybe… both?

name: The Not-So-Abandoned Town of Zaña
description: After an epic flood washed away its enslavers, this city has been part ghost town, part cultural wildcard for the past three centuries.

name: Plaza de Armas
description: Built on the remains of one the Incas’ most important gathering spots, this square keeps the spirit of a fallen empire alive.

name: Tombs of Sipán
description: The shockingly unspoiled Peruvian tomb of the Lord of Sipan was once guarded by a footless warrior.

name: Cerro Sechin
description: The depictions of slaughter are surprisingly detailed at this mysterious ancient site.

name: Aramu Muru
description: An abandoned Incan construction project left behind a mysterious "doorway."

name: Puquios
description: The sophisticated hydraulic system of the Nazca civilization.

name: Parque de la Papa
description: A highland eco-park celebrates more than a thousand varieties of tuber.

name: Tres Chimbadas Lake
description: This small oxbow lake has rare giant river otters and some Seussian-sounding birds.

name: El Cacaotal
description: Visit Lima’s one-stop-shop for sustainably sourced and produced Peruvian chocolate.

name: Pampa Galeras National Reserve
description: This high-Andean reserve protects the graceful vicuña that was nearly hunted to extinction for its precious wool.

name: Taquile Island
description: On this tiny spot of land in the middle of Lake Titicaca the only ones who do the knitting are the men.

name: Castillo de Lamas
description: A kitschy Italian medieval-style castle incongruously located amidst one of the most historic towns in the Peruvian Amazon.

name: Museo del Juguete
description: Peru’s only toy museum houses everything from pre-Inca whistles to lead soldiers and terrifying dolls.

name: Petroglyphs of Polish
description: This set of mysterious petroglyphs in the Peruvian jungle could shed light on centuries-old activities in the region.

name: Chucuito Temple of Fertility
description: Ancient Peruvian fertility temple or modern money-making hoax? Either way these monoliths still look like penises.

name: Rumicolca
description: The Incas turned this ancient Wari aqueduct into an imposing defensive gate.

name: Cumbe Mayo and Los Frailones
description: Pre-Incan aqueduct amidst a stone forest in Peru.

name: Huaca de la Luna
description: The outside of this exposed Moche pyramid is covered with striking colorful adobe friezes.

name: Jicamarca Ionospheric Radio Observatory
description: Enormous array of antennae fill a desert valley.

name: The Paredones
description: These overlooked ruins give an intimate look at the Inca Empire's urban planning prowess.

name: Machu Picchu Discovery Plaques
description: It took 82 years and four different plaques to finally get the story right.

name: Lares Hot Springs
description: Bathe in these natural hot springs located high in the Andes Mountains.

name: Cripta de los Héroes
description: The elaborate crypt where Peruvian heroes of the War of the Pacific were laid to rest.

name: Plaza de Toros de Acho
description: This bullring in Lima is the oldest in the Americas and the second-oldest in the world.

name: Area 21 Cusco
description: An extraterrestrial-themed art exhibit makes the perfect place to crash-land while interacting with Andean culture and history.

name: Coricancha
description: A Christian monastery stands on what was once a holy Incan temple.

name: Abtao Submarine Museum
description: The retired vessel is South America's first floating museum.

name: Apachetas of Chivay
description: Mystic Incan rock piles three miles above sea level.

name: Cats of Parque Kennedy
description: In the upscale neighborhood of Miraflores in Lima, a pretty little park is home to dozens of cats.

name: Ceremonial Center of Pamashto
description: A circle of stones on a hilltop in Peru’s high jungle, aligned with the winter solstice and largely ignored by archaeologists.

name: Pyramids of Paratoari
description: Mysterious "dots" seen from a satellite in southeast Peru.

name: Sillustani
description: These ruined Incan mausoleum towers still bear carvings of sacred animals on their blocks.

name: Paseo Yortuque
description: Stroll among dozens of statues depicting pre-Columbian kings, gods, and supernatural beings.

name: Museo Antonini
description: This archaeological museum in one of Peru’s most intriguing regions exhibits ancient artifacts from the Nazca civilization.

name: Monasterio de Santa Catalina
description: This 16th-century monastery was founded to cloister the daughters of wealthy local families.

name: Toro Muerto
description: Thousands of volcanic rocks carved 1,000 years ago.

name: Pozo de Yanayacu
description: This well held a sacred source of water supposedly unleashed from the barren rocks by a wizard-like archbishop.

name: The Former Wiñay Wayna Pub
description: This is why we can't have nice things on the Incan Trail.

name: La Rinconada Goldmine
description: In this mine all the gold you can carry may not be as sweet of a deal as it sounds.

name: Ica Stones at the Cabrera Museum
description: Peru's (not so) mysterious carved stones.

name: Francisco Pizarro Statue
description: A monument to the conquistador became increasingly controversial in the very city that he founded.

name: Giant Sombrero of Celendin
description: In a town known for its large hats, this one is the biggest.

name: Grave of Carlos Fitzcarrald
description: The forgotten tomb of the Peruvian rubber baron who was the inspiration for Werner Herzog's epic film "Fitzcarraldo."

name: Maria Reiche Museum
description: A museum dedicated to the German scientist who revealed the significance of the mysterious Peruvian Nazca Lines.

name: Steamship Yavari
description: The 19th-century vessel floats proudly on Lake Titicaca, but its journey from Britain to the Peruvian altiplano was far from smooth sailing.

name: Puerto Maldonado Obelisco
description: This space-age looking obelisk rises over a city in the rainforest, meant to resemble the local castaña tree.

name: Fortaleza del Real Felipe
description: Haunted by its violent past, this colonial bastion was the largest Spanish military installation in the Americas.

name: Jardín de Picaflores Inkamazonia
description: The Peruvian Andes are a center of hummingbird diversity, and this garden offers an opportunity to see more than 30 species of hummingbirds.

name: Pyramids of Caral-Supe
description: A 5,000-year-old city in Peru holds the remains of a stunning set of astronomically-aligned pyramids.

name: Machu Colca
description: This archaeological site features an early version of today’s refrigerators.

name: ‘Abode of the Gods’
description: This outdoor sculpture gallery is a modern-day homage to Andean culture and history.

name: Paseo de la Cultura Mochica
description: This walk to the Pacific Ocean is surrounded by modern statues of Mochica Gods.

name: Añashuayco Quarry
description: An outdoor gallery of sculptures chiseled from a unique stone.

name: Volcán Pabellones
description: Standing 26 feet high and 13 feet in diameter, is this the world’s smallest volcano?

name: The Seven-Headed Palm Tree
description: A twisted, six-headed palm tree cursed by a witch, said to spell calamity if its seventh head is allowed to grow.

name: Cruz del Cóndor
description: A lookout spot for the Andean condor, at the edges of one of the deepest canyons in the world.

name: Santa Barbara Mine Ruins
description: Once the largest source of mercury in the Americas, these ruins earned the name “Mine of Death.”

name: Miculla Petroglyphs
description: In the desert of southern Peru is an intriguing collection of ancient petroglyphs whose origins remain unknown.

name: Casa de Fierro
description: This iron building in the Amazon allegedly shares an architect with the Eiffel Tower.

name: Ayapua Boat Museum
description: In a past life this steamboat hauled rubber and passengers up and down the Amazon.

name: Museo de la Memoria (Museum of Memory)
description: A house once used to feed orphans of a brutal conflict now serves as a museum honoring its victims.

name: Convento de Santa Rosa de Ocopa
description: Once the center of the largest missionary operation in South America, this convent now hosts a museum and library.

name: Expreso Virgen de Guadalupe
description: This antique rail car has been reborn as an urban cafe.

name: The Diana Room
description: A small, emotionally charged room inside the Mario Testino Museum dedicated to Princess Diana and one famous photo shoot.
